description: Create kernel/evolution/historian.py for pruning, summarizing, and analyzing evolution history. Integrate auto-prune into feedback_loop.
steps: Create kernel/evolution/historian.py with class EvolutionHistorian: (a) __init__(history_file: Path, archive_dir: Path), (b) summarize_history() -> dict with stats: total_changes, applied_count, rejected_count, rolled_back_count, success_rate, most_modified_nodes (top 3), most_reverted_types, (c) prune_history(max_entries=500) that moves old entries to archive_dir/archive_YYYYMMDD.jsonl and keeps only the last max_entries in history.jsonl, (d) analyze_effectiveness() -> dict mapping change_type to {applied, reverted, stick_rate}, (e) get_evolution_velocity(window=10) -> float measuring changes per window iterations

Modify kernel/feedback_loop.py: add auto_prune flag to __init__ (default True). In run_cycle(), after recording metrics, if auto_prune is True, check history length and prune if > 500 entries. Import and use EvolutionHistorian.

Modify feedback_loop.py run_cycle(): before generating proposals, call analyze_effectiveness(). If a proposal's type has stick_rate < 0.3, reduce its confidence_score by 0.3 (min 0) to avoid proposing change types that historically get reverted

Create tests/test_historian.py with tests: summarize with various history data, prune creates archive and trims history, analyze_effectiveness computes correct rates, velocity calculation, integration with feedback_loop auto-prune
Run tests to confirm everything passes
acceptance_criteria: summarize_history returns correct stats from history.jsonl data
prune_history moves old entries to archive/ and keeps max_entries
analyze_effectiveness correctly computes stick_rate per change type
feedback_loop auto-prunes when history exceeds 500 entries
Proposals with historically low stick_rate get reduced confidence
All new tests pass with coverage >= 90%
findings: All 26 historian tests pass. Full suite 767 tests pass at 98.92% coverage. historian.py at 99% coverage, feedback_loop.py at 97% coverage.
